name: barbell reverse grip skullcrusher
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold the barbell with a reverse grip, palms facing towards your face, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows in and your wrists straight.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench. Hold the barbell with a reverse grip, palms facing towards your face, and your hands shoulder-width apart. Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows in and your wrists straight. Slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
